content:
Recently I ended up going for OE on Corposant, and I noticed that her OE doesn't actually reduce the cost of Lightning Retribution by 2, even though it says Aftershock effects apply twice.

This isn't *actually* a bug, but rather an interaction that has to do with how the card is worded: In order to keep Lightning Retribution in hand through discards, the Retain tag is not enough, and because all the discard effects we have in the game draw cards, the game settles for making the aftershock effect put it at the top of the draw pile and immediately drawing it again when you play a discard effect. 
Then, the Prepare 1 effect activates as a byproduct, effectively acting as a pseudo-retain that lowers its cost.

Normally, when one sees Corpo OE's double aftershock effect, the obvious expectation is that you'd be able to reduce Lightning Retribution's cost by 2 with it, but the aftershock effect that triggers twice is not the prepare 1 effect, as it's not listed under aftershock, but rather the "This card returns to the top of the draw pile" effect, which does nothing for the cost.

I'd like to suggest changing it so that there is a clause that lets the Prepare effect activate twice on Corpo's OE, maybe exclusively for Corpo's cards, as well as it's current effect, so the OE can be used as intended for her own biggest discard target.

Below I've attached a video of the phenomenon happening, and it can be seen pretty clearly how awkward it feels when everything else gets double aftershock *except* the one thing you're looking to trigger Aftershock on the most:

https://youtu.be/qiiMszeYvas

Thanks in advance!

embeds.title: corpo OE bug
embeds.description: aftershock is supposed to apply twice, but lightning retribution cost only gets reduced by 1 arithmetica due to how the card is worded

content: would be nice to atleast get a clarification on how this is supposed to work if its not bugged
Based on its wording, it is working properly, but it is quite weird that her OE, which is an amazing effect, does nothing for her strongest card
yep
I recognize that it's not a bug but rather a byproduct of how it's worded
but i think in the "spirit of the game"
it definitely should affect it
-2 cost to lightning is the first thing that comes to most people's minds when you look at her OE triggering double aftershocks, I think

Definitely what I thought until you emphasised what her aftershock actually did, and yeah, putting it on top of the deck twice doesn't actually do anything
And tbh, less in the spirit of the game, and more so just a slight 'buff' that honestly felt like it should've been always like that
let her prep her 4 cost twice, it's not a big ask
GHelot gets Prep 2 at E2. This is OE, for a single card.
OE is expensive, and while I don't regret it, I don't think it would be too unreasonable to make sure the OE reduces the cost by 2 when it comes to her power budget
I think a fix for it wouldn't even need a special clause. Just add aftershock: "reduce cost by 1"
cuz it already has prepare 1, and I can see not wanting it be a prep 2
wait, no, fk, that won't work, will it, that'd reduce by 3
double reduce 1 and prep 1 = 3
the cleanest solution I thought of was adding the clause to the OE to trigger a prepare effect when used, but only on her cards

that way it doesnt intervene with any other prepare effects, doesnt make her stronger when it comes to supporting units with prepare, and doesn't mess with the base card or base exalt unless you have the 200 aliemus needed to cast the actual OE
It will be a considerable buff for DoM + Saya duping shenanigans Corpo, tho
then again, OE, so, fk it
yeah, if that's the case, I think it's okay
OE is expensive to roll

currently, our lineup of discard units with aftershock effects is just okay
the OE has really good value but it honestly doesn't do anything crazy either
the card I want aftershock to trigger the most on is still Lightning Retribution
I think a Saya+DoM buff would be fine, and within her power budget, especially if it comes alongside making expectations for what her OE does line up closer with what it *actually* should do

(PvP Bug Exploitation and Community Management Concern 1/3)
Dear Morimens Team,

Recently, I noticed that in the Discord PvP Chinese channel, some players have publicly shared content showing the use of a game bug in PvP, and displayed the abnormal results achieved through this method. They have even encouraged other players to imitate and use the same method. These discussions have already led some players to believe that such behavior is acceptable, or even something worth promoting. Similar content has also appeared and spread on other social platforms.

Based on my current observations, this bug has already had a noticeable impact on PvP balance. For example, this bug can enable FTK situations that were previously almost impossible to achieve, allowing some players to gain an unfair competitive advantage and affecting the gameplay experience and sense of fairness for other players participating in PvP normally.

In the channel, I publicly expressed my opposition to using bugs in PvP and stated that openly promoting such behavior was inappropriate. However, instead of a reasonable discussion focused on rules and competitive fairness, my viewpoint received dismissive and hostile responses from some participants. More importantly, one of the participants publicly stated:

「事實是沒人會理你，我們依舊爽，不爽把貓或工作人員叫來啊。你不敢是不是？那我幫你。」

(Translation: "The fact is that nobody will care about you. We will continue enjoying it. If you are unhappy, call Light or the staff. You don't dare to, right? Then I will help you.")

After that, the participant actively used the @ function to mention staff members. I also used @ to bring this discussion to the attention of the official team, hoping that the developers could be aware of the situation and provide clarification.

As of now, I have not seen any response from staff members in the channel, nor any related moderation action regarding this matter.

(PvP Bug Exploitation and Community Management Concern 2/3)
I am not making this post to pressure the team to immediately fix the bug. Investigation, confirmation, and fixing of bugs require time, and I fully understand that.

However, it is important to clarify that:

Whether a bug can be fixed immediately and whether players should intentionally exploit that bug to gain an advantage in PvP are two separate issues.

Therefore, I hope the official team can clearly explain what the current rules and stance are regarding intentionally using bugs in PvP.

I would appreciate an official response to the following questions:

• Does the official team consider intentionally using known bugs to gain a PvP advantage acceptable behavior?

• Does the official team consider publicly spreading, promoting, or encouraging others to use bugs within the community acceptable?

• For bug exploitation that affects competitive fairness, as well as discussions that publicly promote such behavior within official community spaces, will the official team take appropriate moderation actions?

(PvP Bug Exploitation and Community Management Concern 3/3)
At this point, what concerns me most is not only the bug itself, but also the perception that has started to appear within the community: that publicly exploiting bugs will not result in any consequences. Some players have even openly stated that staff members will not intervene and have actively invited staff members to witness such behavior.

If this perception remains unaddressed for a long period of time, it may lead players to believe that such behavior is implicitly accepted by the official team, further affecting the PvP environment, community order, and increasing the possibility of similar behavior happening again.

Even if the bug has not yet been fixed, the official team can still clearly communicate its stance regarding intentionally exploiting bugs for competitive advantage and provide players with clear rules and expectations.

The main purpose of this post is to request clarification on whether intentionally exploiting bugs to gain an advantage in PvP is considered acceptable under the game rules, and how the official team plans to maintain a fair competitive environment.

For verification purposes, the complete discussion of this incident is still available in the Discord PvP Chinese channel. I have also saved some relevant screenshots in case the content is later deleted or modified.

I hope the official team can provide a clear response regarding this matter and explain the principles for handling PvP bug exploitation and related community management issues.

Thank you.

(This post was assisted by AI for wording and organization. The events, opinions, and related evidence are provided by me.)

《morimens》运营组您好：

近期，我注意到在 Discord 的 PvP 中文频道中，有玩家公开分享展示在 PvP 中利用游戏 Bug 的内容，并展示通过该方式获得的非正常结果。他们甚至鼓励其他玩家模仿并使用相同的方法。这些讨论已经导致部分玩家认为此类行为是可以接受的，甚至是值得推广的。类似内容也已经出现在其他社交平台并被传播。

根据目前的观察，该 Bug 已经对 PvP 平衡造成了明显影响。例如，该 Bug 可以实现过去几乎无法达成的 FTK 情况，使部分玩家能够获得不公平的竞技优势，并影响正常参与 PvP 的其他玩家的游戏体验以及对公平性的感受。

在频道中，我曾公开表达反对在 PvP 中使用 Bug，并认为公开推广此类行为是不恰当的。然而，我的观点并没有得到围绕规则和竞技公平性的合理讨论，反而收到了一些参与者的否定性回应和敌意回应。更重要的是，其中一名参与者公开表示：

「事實是沒人會理你，我們依舊爽，不爽把貓或工作人員叫來啊。你不敢是不是？那我幫你。」

之后，该参与者主动使用 @ 功能提及工作人员。我也进行了 @，希望让官方团队注意到此次讨论，并希望开发团队能够了解情况并提供说明。

截至目前，我没有看到频道内有工作人员对此事作出回应，也没有看到针对该事件的相关管理措施。

我发起此帖并不是为了要求团队立即修复该 Bug。Bug 的调查、确认以及修复都需要时间，这一点我完全理解。

但是，需要明确的是：

Bug 是否能够被立即修复，以及玩家是否应该故意利用该 Bug 在 PvP 中获得优势，是两个不同的问题。

因此，我希望官方团队能够明确说明，目前对于在 PvP 中故意使用 Bug 的相关规则和态度是什么。

希望官方能够回应以下问题：

• 官方团队是否认为，故意利用已知 Bug 获取 PvP 优势属于可以接受的行为？

• 官方团队是否认为，在社区中公开传播、推广或鼓励他人使用 Bug 的行为属于可以接受的行为？

• 对于影响竞技公平性的 Bug 利用行为，以及在官方社区空间中公开推广此类行为的讨论，官方团队是否会采取适当的管理措施？

目前，我最担忧的不仅是 Bug 本身，还有社区中已经开始出现的一种认知：公开利用 Bug 不会导致任何后果。一些玩家甚至公开表示工作人员不会介入，并主动邀请工作人员见证此类行为。

如果这种认知长期得不到解决，可能会导致玩家认为此类行为实际上被官方默认接受，从而进一步影响 PvP 环境、社区秩序，并增加类似行为再次发生的可能性。

即使该 Bug 尚未修复，官方团队仍然可以明确表达对于为了竞技优势而故意利用 Bug 行为的态度，并向玩家提供清晰的规则和预期。

本帖的主要目的，是希望官方明确说明：根据游戏规则，在 PvP 中故意利用 Bug 获取优势是否属于可接受行为，以及官方团队计划如何维护公平的竞技环境。

为便于核实，此次事件的完整讨论目前仍可以在 Discord PvP 中文频道查看。我也保存了一些相关截图，以防后续内容被删除或修改。

希望官方团队能够对此事给予明确回应，并说明对于 PvP Bug 利用行为以及相关社区管理问题的处理原则。

谢谢。

（本文由 AI 协助整理措辞和结构。事件经过、观点以及相关证据均由本人提供。）
Could you share the message links to the explanations or examples of this bug? And the person that pinged the staff

Here is a video link showing an example of the bug, with a Chinese explanation included.https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1fGNh6AEjy Basically, the bug is that ithe wheel  cannot correctly copy the skill card that was just drawn by effect . Instead, it copies the last card drawn before that.The specific individuals involved can be easily found in the PvP Chinese channel. The purpose of this post is to discuss community standards and official policies rather than to publicly target specific players, so I do not believe naming individuals directly in this post is necessary at this time.
